self_description: I work in mental health and keep noticing how much people's language reveals about recurring patterns. I am curious about AI for self-reflection, but I worry about dependency, false certainty, and systems pretending to be therapists. I often repeat the same client education about those boundaries and would like to make it easier to understand.

possibilities.label: Boundary guide
possibilities.why_fit: You repeatedly explain where safe AI reflection ends and therapy begins.
possibilities.what_they_do: Shape a plain-language interactive client guide that distinguishes reflection prompts, warning signs, and professional support.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: A one-page branching guide built from anonymized examples, with clear safety language and no clinical claims.

possibilities.label: Language pattern lab
possibilities.why_fit: You are drawn to recurring language patterns but cautious about false certainty.
possibilities.what_they_do: Design a local explorer for anonymized sample writing that highlights repeated phrases while separating observation from interpretation.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: A tiny offline prototype using invented text and a visible limitations panel.

possibilities.label: Dependency map
possibilities.why_fit: Your concern about AI self-reflection is not only whether it works, but how reliance changes over time.
possibilities.what_they_do: Map helpful uses, escalation risks, and questions a person can ask before turning to AI again.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: A compact decision map suitable for discussion or client education.

possibilities.label: Surprise me
possibilities.why_fit: This combines your interest in language with your wish to communicate safe boundaries without pretending to diagnose anyone.
possibilities.what_they_do: Create a choose-your-own-reflection story where wording changes what the reader notices, followed by a transparent debrief.
possibilities.first_10_30_minutes: One short interactive scene plus a safety-focused explanation of what the exercise can and cannot reveal.
